proposals.caveat: Requires a clean set of peer MSAs with verified no-event weeks and good seasonal matching.
proposals.question: In weeks when a city hosts a home game, do matched peer MSAs without any events show no comparable spending increase, supporting a causal home-game effect?
proposals.rationale: Cross-MSA placebo comparisons by week help rule out national shocks and seasonality using only the local event calendar and weekly spending series.

proposals.caveat: Thresholds can be tuned and holiday-adjacent weeks may need exclusion.
proposals.question: Which MSAs have at least 8 home-event weeks and 20 clean non-event weeks in the sample, enabling robust within-MSA event-versus-baseline estimates?
proposals.rationale: A coverage screen ensures enough treated and control weeks per MSA to support precise comparisons with the available city/MSA weekly event and spending data.

proposals.caveat: Interpretation requires separating weeks with back-to-back events to avoid contamination of the post period.
proposals.question: Does the spending lift in event weeks reverse in the following week within the same city, indicating intertemporal substitution rather than net growth?
proposals.rationale: A within-city t−1, t, t+1 design on weekly data can test persistence of effects using only the local event calendar and spending series.
